chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ يُوصِي بِحَجَّةٍ فَيُحَجُّ عَنْهُ مِنْ غَيْرِ مَوْضِعِهِ أَوْ يُوصِي بِشَيْ‏ءٍ قَلِيلٍ فِي الْحَجِّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 62 – The one who bequeaths with a Hajj, so he performs Hajj from other than its place, or he bequeaths something little regarding the Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/220)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/220)
text: رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى أَنْ يُحَجَّ عَنْهُ حَجَّةُ الْإِسْلَامِ فَلَمْ يَبْلُغْ جَمِيعُ مَا تَرَكَ إِلَّا خَمْسِينَ دِرْهَماً قَالَ يُحَجُّ عَنْهُ مِنْ بَعْضِ الْأَوْقَاتِ الَّتِي وَقَّتَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِنْ قُرْبٍ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Mahmoun, from Ibn Raib,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws regarding a man who bequeaths that Hajj be performed on his behalf, the Hajj of Al-Islam, but the entirety of what he left did not reach (the required amount), except for fifty Dirhams. Heasws said: ‘He should perform Hajj on his behalf from one of the junctures which Rasool-Allahsaww used as a juncture (to wear Ihraam), from nearby’.
en.sarwar: 4. Ahmad ibn Muhammad from has narrated from ibn Mahbub from ibn Ri’ab who has said the following: “About a man who dies and in his will says to perform Hajjahta al-Islam for him, but his legacy is not sufficient. It is altogether fifty dirham. Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘It must be performed for him from any one of the Miqat (place for assuming the state of Ihram) from which the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, assumed the state of Ihram.’”
